Here Ballinger turned to the sub
ject of arid lands, and conservation
of natural resources.


Reclamation is a phase of our
national development which is com
handing more and more attention.
he said; 'one of the big things. in
fact. There is nothing that the gov
eminent has undertaken which has
accomplished so much good in sc
short a time.


THE tide of population Is flow
ing westward. These immense
tracts of arid land must be re-
claimed for the new settlers. The
policy of the government is to en-
large our area of producing soil
1t is clear that the incoming admin
istration will do whatever it can to
accomplish this purpose.


Il feel sure, also, that the admin.
istration will continue the policies
of conservation of great natural re.
sources of the west along practical
lines. There are the forests and
water rights; minerals, such as
coal, and the soils. All of these
must be protected. Public policy
demands ii. We must look out for
